title: Characterisation of lung function trajectories and associated early-life predictors in an Australian birth cohort study.
ehr_evidence: The study uses data from the Raine Study, a prospective pregnancy cohort, rather than an EHR or linked health database.
summary: A longitudinal study characterizing lung function trajectories (FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC) from 6 to 22 years of age in an Australian birth cohort to identify early-life risk factors.
key_findings: Four lung function trajectories identified for FEV1, FVC, and FEV1/FVC.
Asthma, maternal smoking, and early-life PM2.5 exposure were identified as risk factors for low lung function trajectories.
population: 1512 children from the Raine Study (Western Australia)
limitations: Specific to a single birth cohort
